text: Last class you said: I started, I studied, I joined, I moved, I applied.
Today some verbs change shape: go becomes went, and get becomes got.
Go
Get
Make
Leave
Become
Take
Win
Find
I went to a conference in 2015.
She got a great job at Sanofi.
We made a big decision.
They left the company in 2019.
David, what was the biggest moment in your career?
In 2010 I got my first job, and I found my passion.
That is great. What did you do next?
I made a big decision. I left that job and went to Sao Paulo.
And your biggest win?
I became a manager, and my team won a big award.
I got my first job in 2010.
I made a big decision and left.
I went to a new city for the role.
I became a manager at Sanofi.
Last year my team won an award.

Hi, I am Paulo, and I want to share the key moments of my career. In 2010 I got my first real job at a small company. Two years later, I made a big decision and left that job for a new challenge. I went to another city, and I became a junior manager there. The work was hard, but I took every chance to learn. In 2017 my team won an important award, and I found a new passion for leadership. Those moments changed everything. The lesson I learned is simple: every career has a few key moments, and you must be brave when they come.

Hello, my name is Lucia, and these are the turning points of my story. After university I got a job in a laboratory, but it was not my dream. One day I made a choice: I left the lab and went back to study. I became a teacher, and I found real joy in that work. A few years later, I took a leadership role at a big school. My team won a national prize for our project. Each key moment taught me something new. My advice is this: when a turning point comes, take it, because it can change your whole life.

First, she got her first job at a small company. Then she made a big decision and left for a new role. After that, she went to another city and became a manager. Her team won an important award that year. Finally, she found a new passion and took a leadership role.
